seoTitle: How to Use TradingView Bar Replay for Free in 2026
metaDescription: Learn how to use TradingView's Bar Replay for free, fix it when it's greyed out or not working, and find alternatives if your plan limits access.
intro: Bar Replay lets you rewind the market and practice reading charts or testing strategies as if you were trading in real time. This guide explains how to activate it on TradingView's desktop and mobile apps, what free accounts can and can't do with it, and what to try when the feature appears greyed out or restricted. It also points to alternative tools if your plan doesn't give full access.

steps.heading: Activate Bar Replay on desktop or mobile
steps.body: Turn on Bar Replay from within a TradingView chart to start simulating past market conditions and practicing trade decisions.

steps.heading: Know what free accounts can access
steps.body: Free TradingView plans allow some Bar Replay functionality but restrict certain features that are reserved for premium subscribers.

steps.heading: Fix Bar Replay when it's greyed out
steps.body: If Bar Replay appears unavailable, it's often tied to plan limits rather than a bug, so check your subscription level before troubleshooting further.

steps.heading: Try alternatives if access is limited
steps.body: When the free plan's restrictions get in the way, consider custom indicators, scripts, or other platforms that offer similar replay functionality without upgrading.

faq.question: Is Bar Replay free on TradingView?
faq.answer: Bar Replay has some free-tier availability, but full functionality is generally reserved for premium plans.

faq.question: Why is Bar Replay not working or greyed out for me?
faq.answer: This usually happens because certain Bar Replay features are limited to paid TradingView subscriptions.

faq.question: Can I simulate trades without upgrading to premium?
faq.answer: Yes, custom indicators, scripts, or alternative platforms can approximate similar replay functionality on a free account.

faq.question: What is Bar Replay used for?
faq.answer: It lets traders rewind the chart to a past point and practice strategies or chart reading as if trading in real time.
